statement: When a Copilot workspace includes technology-specific instruction files, audit research should include domain content research alongside Copilot mechanics research — an instruction file can be structurally correct and content-empty, and only domain knowledge can identify that gap.

evidenceRefs.title: r/cpp Design Patterns thread (83 upvotes) — practitioner consensus on what C++ design guidance should substantively cover
r/cpp Software Architecture and Design thread (88 upvotes) — practitioner consensus on what architecture guidance should include
C++ Core Guidelines — the benchmark for what substantive C++ instruction content should align with

publicSummary: Copilot audit research for projects with technology-specific instruction files should evaluate domain content quality against domain baselines, not only validate file structure — a correctly-formatted but content-empty instruction file is still an audit gap.
